Any unit may be summoned by calling the Laboratory Emergency Control Center (phone 3-6358) or the Laboratory Emergency Director (phone 3-6606).
A given emergency may require the evacuation of (1) all MSRE personnel or (2) only nonoperating personnel.
1) and await further instructions from the Local Emergency Supervisor.

If the evacuation was initiated by a radiation alarm and if immediate instructions are not forthcoming from the Local Emergency Supervisor, all personnel will proceed as rapidly as possible along Melton Valley Drive in the upwind or crosswind direction (east or west, whichever more closely agrees with the direction pointed to by the weathervane located on top of the pole approximately 50 ft north of east end of the office building, 7509).

Nonoperating personnel must evacuate any area when level is $\geq 7.5$ mrem/hr on the Monitrons or $\geq 1000$ counts/min on the CAM's.
Continuous exposure (40-hr week) $\leqslant 2\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^3$

In view of the possible serious consequences to personnel, the simultaneous occurrence of symptoms 1, 2, and 3 should be treated as a maximum credible accident unless absolute assurance to the contrary is available.

section: PERSONS TEMPORARILY ASSIGNED TO THE MSRE AREA
text: The following persons are authorized to enter the MSRE Area at any time prior to 1600, May 11, 1965.

section: PERSONS TEMPORARILY ASSIGNED TO THE MSRE AREA
text: If a vehicle must enter or exit through the North Gate or West Gate, the MSRE Shift Supervisor will call the Guard Department (phone 3-6646), and a guard will come and unlock the gate.
The normal power for the intercom phones comes from the TVA line (panel 2, Auxiliary Control Room).
Backup power is available from the 250-v battery station and from the diesel-generator station.

A system is provided to permit the Laboratory Emergency Director, Building 5000, or the Emergency Control Center, Building 2500, to make announcements or to sound emergency signals (see Part IV-B of ORNL Emergency Manual) over the MSRE PA system.
The circuit to the MSRE also includes the other Melton Valley facilities (HFIR, Tower Shielding, 7500, HPRR).
Fixed monitors, which require a 110-v ac power supply, are stationed at specific, strategic locations.
The Control Room alarm setup includes a system of lights to denote various instrument conditions (see Table 7.1).

<sup>a</sup>Caution level for a beta-gamma air monitor is 1000 counts/min and for a Monitron is 7.5 mr/hr.
bHigh level for a beta-gamma air monitor is 4000 counts/min; and for a Monitron, $23\mathrm{mr / hr}$
Fourteen G-M tubes and 12 ion chamber detectors are used for process monitoring.

Each stream is withdrawn from the stack at a point about 40 ft above the base, passes through the monitoring (or collecting) device and the sample pump, and is then returned to the stack near the base (see Fig.
The signals from the count ratemeters (displayed and recorded in the MSRE Auxiliary Control Room) are transmitted by telemetry to recorders in the Waste Monitoring Control Center at Building 3105.
The recorders are monitored visually on a 24-hr basis by Laboratory Facilities personnel.

One bottle at $\geq 1500$ psig contains sufficient gas to operate the horn for approximately 4 minutes.

The maximum permissible concentration for acute exposure for workers without respiratory protection should not exceed $25\mu \mathrm{g}$ of Be per cubic meter and the time limit for such exposure should be less than 30 min.
Chronic Exposure Level. $\leqslant 2\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^3$ for an 8-hr, 5-day week.
Environmental Air Concentration Levels. $0.01 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^3$ for in-plant or "neighborhood" samples.
Walls, floors, and equipment within the beryllium control area, $\leqslant 25\mu \mathrm{g}$ per 12 in.².
For equipment being transferred to a nonberyllium control area, $\leqslant 4\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{ft}^2$ .
The reactor cell is maintained at -2 psig by bleeding inleakage to the stack from the component coolant pump discharge.
A minimum face velocity of 100-ft/min is required through the opening into any contaminated cell.

Actuation of a manual station or an automatic detector transmits a signal to the fire alarm zone indicator and control cabinet (located at the north end of the Building 7503 office corridor), which, in turn, causes the following action:
Water supply for the sprinkler system comes from the potable water main (840-ft level, north end of Building 7503).
A connection is also provided on the north side of Building 7503 (852-ft level, outside) for a supplementary water supply (see Part I of this report).
Six hose cabinets (four on the 852-ft level and two on the 840-ft level) plus strategically located portable extinguishers provide additional fire-fighting capacity.
Each hose cabinet is equipped with 75 ft of hose and a 15-lb $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ extinguisher.
